title: WebAssembly Binary
documentation:
## Purpose
Carries a compiled WebAssembly binary module. Wasm binaries are stack-machine bytecode that run in sandboxed browser environments and standalone runtimes (Wasmtime, WAMR, WasmEdge, Node.js). They are the compilation target for C, C++, Rust, Go, and other languages.

## When to use
Use `media:wasm` when a pipeline node executes, instruments, or analyzes a compiled Wasm binary — for example, a Wasmtime execution step, a binary analysis tool using `wasm-tools`, or a deployment step uploading a module to an edge runtime.

## Notes
Wasm binaries are not human-readable; for readable text representation, use [WebAssembly Text](https://capdag.com/browse/media:wat;textable;code) (`.wat`). The Wasm binary format begins with the magic bytes `\0asm` followed by a 4-byte version field. See also [WebAssembly Binary](https://capdag.com/browse/media:wasm).
